danger_signals.visual_form: Rats and hostile creatures in level spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat; player must avoid or defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Cheese mines and Stiltonium extractors visually marked in environments
danger_signals.meaning: Destructible objectives tied to antagonist's power; targeting these reduces threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Custard-themed level sections with hazardous liquid visuals
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage; requires Fruit Suit protection or avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Dynamite and bombs as pickable items with visible fuses/timers
danger_signals.meaning: Dual-use danger: tool for destruction but damages player if detonated nearby

danger_signals.visual_form: Ceiling traversal sections (magno-boots mechanic); inverted platforming spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental complexity; player can fall if not managing upside-down navigation

safety_signals.visual_form: Fruit Suit pickup icon; visual appearance change when equipped
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability; absorbs 2+ enemy hits or allows passage through hazardous liquids

safety_signals.visual_form: Spring shoes, jetpack, X-ray specs as findable icons in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced mobility and capability; expands safe traversal options

safety_signals.visual_form: Finnius icon pickup; visual transition between James Pond and Finnius Frog
safety_signals.meaning: Alternative mechanic access; Finnius offers bouncing mobility not available to Pond

safety_signals.visual_form: Beacon structures visibly placed in each level
safety_signals.meaning: Level objective completion marker; destroying these advances mission progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden pickups that reveal secret level pathways upon collection
safety_signals.meaning: Access to optional content; essential for full game completion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: F.I.5.H agency branding (Operation Starfish stylized as Operation Starfi5h)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Moon setting; themed level sections (cheese, custard, ice cream) representing infiltration targets

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cheese mines and Stiltonium extractors as visual markers of his operations
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rat workforce as visual representation of hired labor/minions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mining infrastructure; cheese production facilities; superweapon construction sites (Stiltonium extractors)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Finnius icon/face identifier for character switching
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rescue location within levels; signifies ally availability once freed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Themed level zones (cheese, custard, ice cream) depicting resource extraction; visible mining infrastructure throughout environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rat enemies actively patrolling and defending extraction sites; cheese mines operational

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Active Stiltonium extractors visible in certain level sections
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game does not provide full ending until all cheese mines AND Stiltonium extractors are destroyed; signals ongoing threat

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Level progression gated by beacon destruction
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Communication beacons prominently placed; some require four colored teacups collected before activation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players must gather prerequisite items before beacons become destructible; signals checkpoint control mechanism

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ceiling environments accessible via magno-boots; upside-down level layout visuals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate against standard gravity; falling direction reversed unless jumping away from ceiling

environmental_storytelling.example: Garden of Edam (first level) — play on Garden of Eden; contextualizes moon setting as agricultural/paradise transformed into industrial site
Level sections grouped as cheese, custard, ice cream — visual metaphor for Dr. Maybe's profit motive and resource extraction agenda
Cheese mines and Stiltonium extractors visible as destructible structures — player actions directly dismantle antagonist's operations
Hot custard liquids in custard-themed zones — environmental design reinforces thematic consistency and danger communication
Finnius must be rescued in specific level before becoming playable — spatial narrative tied to mechanical availability

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Run, jump, punch; magno-boots enable ceiling traversal
Behavior expands based on gadget type: spring shoes enable repeated bouncing; jetpack enables vertical propulsion; fruit gun enables projectile attacks
Can traverse hazardous liquids safely; absorbs enemy damage
Bounces repeatedly on belly to achieve incrementally higher jumps; cannot use gadgets except chattering teeth
Present throughout Dr. Maybe's mining operations; actively oppose player progress; defeated by punch or projectile attacks
Dynamite ignites immediately upon pickup; bombs detonate only after thrown; both destroy specific blocks and damage player/enemies
Some require four colored teacups collected before becoming destructible; signals gated objective progression

notes: James Pond 3 communicates world-state primarily through environmental theming and objective structure rather than dynamic visual shifts. The moon setting under Dr. Maybe's industrial occupation is expressed through themed level zones (dairy product metaphor) and visible mining/extraction infrastructure. Danger is signaled through enemy presence, environmental hazards (hot liquids, gravity inversion), and destructible structures tied to antagonist power. The game uses gadget acquisition, character switching, and protective suits as safety/capability signals. The narrative of incomplete victory (requiring destruction of both cheese mines AND Stiltonium extractors for full ending) is communicated mechanically rather than visually, making objective clarity dependent on player knowledge or exploration. Faction identity relies on spatial markers (F.I.5.H Moon mission vs. Dr. Maybe's mining operations) rather than color-coded or symbolic systems.
